title: Acute Abdomen - Perforated Peptic Ulcer
chief_complaint: Sudden severe epigastric pain that spread to entire abdomen 4 hours ago. Lies very still as any movement worsens pain. History of NSAID use for arthritis.

physical_exam.abdomen: Board-like rigidity, rebound tenderness, absent bowel sounds

hidden_data.ct_abdomen: Free air and fluid, perforated duodenal ulcer

correct_diagnosis: Perforated Duodenal Ulcer with Generalized Peritonitis
key_learning_points: Free air under diaphragm = perforated viscus until proven

patient_responses.pain_onset: It started suddenly in my stomach and now my whole belly hurts
patient_responses.nsaid: I take diclofenac daily for arthritis

hints.imaging: Look for free air under diaphragm

feedback.correct_message: Excellent! Perforated viscus is a surgical emergency.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is a perforated ulcer. The free air + board-like rigidity are diagnostic.
feedback.attending_note: Perforated ulcer: Resuscitate, broad-spectrum antibiotics, emergency laparotomy with omental patch repair (Graham patch). Time to OR is critical. Good outcome with prompt surgery.
